description: Layout patterns built exclusively with Bootstrap flexbox utilities. Use flex for all alignment and spacing needs. Every layout pattern maps directly to Figma auto-layout properties so Figma MCP output translates without interpretation.

always: Use a layout pattern from this registry for any alignment, spacing, or arrangement need.
Always pair a layout pattern with a gap token — never leave sibling elements without explicit spacing.
Apply layout classes directly to the wrapper element — do not add extra wrapper divs just for spacing.
When reading from Figma MCP, map layoutMode + primaryAxisAlignItems + counterAxisAlignItems to a pattern, then map itemSpacing to a gap token.
never: Do not use CSS Grid for component-level layouts — flexbox only. Grid is reserved for future page-level layouts.
Do not use margin between siblings to create spacing — use gap tokens on the parent instead.
Do not use inline styles for alignment or spacing.
Do not combine two layout pattern classes on the same element.
Do not use gap values outside the three gap tokens (gap-2, gap-3, gap-4).

gapTokens.description: Three gap sizes only. Always use these — never use gap-1, gap-5, or arbitrary spacing values between siblings.

gap-tight.usage: Tightly related items: icon + label, badge inside a heading, chips in a group.

gap-default.usage: Default spacing between most siblings: buttons in a group, fields in a form row, cards side by side.

gap-loose.usage: Sections that need breathing room: major content blocks, large card groups.

row.description: Horizontal layouts. Items sit side by side in a line. Maps to Figma layoutMode: HORIZONTAL.
row.note: Variants are named row-{justify}-{align}. When align is omitted it defaults to 'center'. Use a -top, -bottom, -stretch, or -baseline suffix when children have different heights.

row-start.description: Items left-aligned, vertically centered. Default row layout.
row-start.usage: Navigation items, left-aligned toolbars, icon + label pairs, inline form elements.

row-start-top.description: Items left-aligned, top-aligned. Use when children have different heights and you want them anchored to the top.
row-start-top.usage: Mixed-height cards side by side, icon columns where text wraps, label + multi-line value rows.

row-start-bottom.description: Items left-aligned, bottom-aligned. Use when children should sit on the same baseline at the bottom.
row-start-bottom.usage: Form rows where a taller input sits next to a short label that should align to the bottom.

row-baseline.description: Items aligned so their text baselines line up across the row regardless of font size or height.
row-baseline.usage: Heading + metadata text at different sizes in the same row, currency value next to unit label.

row-center.usage: Centered button groups, empty state actions, hero call-to-action rows.

row-center-top.usage: Centered icon groups where icons have different heights, centered toolbar with mixed components.

row-end.usage: Footer action buttons, right-aligned toolbars, dismiss or close actions.

row-end-top.usage: Right-aligned action buttons where buttons have different heights, top-right corner controls.

row-between.description: First item pushed left, last item pushed right, vertically centered. Classic split layout.
row-between.usage: Card headers (title left, actions right), nav bars, list rows with label and value on opposite sides.

row-between-top.description: First item pushed left, last item pushed right, both top-aligned. Use when split children have different heights.
row-between-top.usage: Card header where title is multiline and the action badge/button is short — both should anchor at the top.
keywords: one left one right top aligned

row-between-stretch.description: First item pushed left, last item pushed right, both stretched to full row height.
row-between-stretch.usage: Split layout where both sides must fill the same height — e.g. a bordered left section and right section.

row-around.description: Equal space distributed around each item (half-space at the edges, full space between items).
row-around.usage: Stat blocks in a row, icon grid rows where items should be loosely distributed.

row-evenly.description: Perfectly equal space between every item and at both edges.
row-evenly.usage: Tab bars, step indicators, evenly distributed nav items.

row-wrap.description: Items sit in a row but wrap to the next line when they run out of space.
row-wrap.usage: Chip groups, badge collections, tag lists, filter pill rows.

row-wrap-top.description: Wrapping row where each row of wrapped items aligns to the top.
row-wrap-top.usage: Mixed-height chips or badges that wrap, form field groups that wrap to a second row.

row-stretch.description: Items left-aligned, each stretched to the full height of the row.
row-stretch.usage: Cards in a row that must share equal height, input groups, button + input combos.

column.description: Vertical layouts. Items stack on top of each other. Maps to Figma layoutMode: VERTICAL.
column.note: Variants are named col-{cross-axis-align} by default with justify-content-start implied. Use col-bottom, col-center-vertical, col-around, or col-evenly to control how the stack distributes vertically inside its container.

col-start.description: Items stacked top to bottom, left-aligned.
col-start.usage: Form field stacks (label above input), left-aligned content groups, sidebar items.

col-center.description: Items stacked top to bottom, centered horizontally.
col-center.usage: Centered empty states, icon + label + action stacked in the middle of the page.

col-end.description: Items stacked top to bottom, right-aligned.
col-end.usage: Right-aligned stacked actions, right-side metadata blocks.

col-stretch.description: Items stacked top to bottom, each stretching to full width of the container. Default column layout.
col-stretch.usage: Card body content, full-width form fields stacked, page section stacks.

col-center-vertical.description: Items centered vertically inside the container, stretching to full width. Use when the column must fill its parent height and the stack should sit in the middle.
col-center-vertical.usage: Vertically centered content inside a fixed-height panel, loading state centered in a card.

col-center-vertical-center.description: Items centered on both axes — vertically and horizontally — inside the container.
col-center-vertical-center.usage: Empty state with icon + message centered inside a card, splash content fully centered.

col-bottom.description: Items pushed to the bottom of the container, stretching to full width.
col-bottom.usage: Footer content pinned to the bottom of a fixed-height panel, action buttons anchored to the bottom of a sidebar.

col-between.description: First item at the top, last item at the bottom, filling the available vertical space.
col-between.usage: Cards with fixed height where header sits at top and footer actions sit at bottom.

col-around.description: Equal space distributed around each stacked item vertically.
col-around.usage: Vertically distributed steps or milestone rows inside a fixed-height container.

col-evenly.description: Perfectly equal vertical space between every item and at top and bottom edges.
col-evenly.usage: Evenly distributed menu items in a fixed-height sidebar, step indicators in a vertical stepper.

figmaMappingGuide.description: When reading Figma MCP output, use this to convert auto-layout properties directly to a layout pattern + gap token. No interpretation needed.
steps: 1. Read layoutMode from the Figma frame (HORIZONTAL or VERTICAL).
2. Read primaryAxisAlignItems (MIN, CENTER, MAX, SPACE_BETWEEN, SPACE_AROUND, SPACE_EVENLY).
3. Read counterAxisAlignItems (MIN, CENTER, MAX, STRETCH, BASELINE).
4. Match to a pattern variant using the table below.
5. Read itemSpacing and map to gap token: 1–10 → gap-tight (gap-2), 11–20 → gap-default (gap-3), 21+ → gap-loose (gap-4).
6. If layoutWrap is WRAP, prefer the row-wrap or row-wrap-top variant regardless of primaryAxisAlignItems.
